inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

reduce_with: `byphoare (_: <pre> ==> <post>)` — to a `phoare` bound on one program
opener_focus.route: reduce `Pr[...] <= Pr[...]` to a relational/phoare judgment first.
opener_focus.yours: the reduction (byequiv vs byphoare), the pre/post, the numeric bound.

checks: Pr resources are context: use them when they match the visible endpoint and simplify the goal.
Direct byequiv is a valid route when the goal is naturally a program equivalence.
